seoTitle: Lake Carnegie vs Lake Clifton — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Lake Carnegie and Lake Clifton. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Lake Carnegie vs Lake Clifton: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Lake Carnegie and Lake Clifton.
heading: Lake Carnegie vs Lake Clifton — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Lake Carnegie, Western Australia and Lake Clifton, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Lake Carnegie has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Lake Clifton (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Lake Carnegie or Lake Clifton safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Lake Carnegie has a flood risk score of 52/100, while Lake Clifton scores 28/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Lake Carnegie scores 37/100 for bushfire risk. Lake Clifton scores 58/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Lake Carnegie or Lake Clifton?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Lake Carnegie has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.73% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
